concepts.title: Call Forwarding Local Survivability
concepts.summary: A survivability feature that redirects inbound calls to Zoom native numbers (or Provider Exchange numbers) through BYOC PSTN numbers when internet connectivity is lost. This ensures users receive calls even during outages by routing through the local PSTN network. Designers should understand this as a pre-configured failover mechanism that admins must plan and activate.
key_points: Automatically forwards Zoom native numbers to BYOC numbers during survivability mode
Inbound calls rerouted through PSTN to local site when Zoom cloud is unreachable
Forwarding rules must be configured before an outage; enabling is a manual step during outage

concepts.title: BYOC (Bring Your Own Carrier) Numbers for Survivability
concepts.summary: Customer-provided PSTN phone numbers that serve as the destination for forwarded calls during survivability mode. The number of BYOC extensions must match the count of unique extensions needed during failover (a 1:1 mapping requirement). One additional BYOC number must be reserved for emergency services (ELIN) and cannot be assigned to any user or entity.
key_points: Must be acquired from BYOC provider and assigned to users within the site
1:1 mapping needed: N users requiring DID equals N BYOC extensions required
One BYOC number must be reserved exclusively for emergency services (ELIN)
Only BYOC numbers assigned to users within the same site can be used

concepts.title: Emergency Location Identification Number (ELIN)
concepts.summary: A dedicated BYOC number required for emergency services support during survivability mode. The ELIN is configured at the Site level and must remain unassigned to any user or Zoom Phone entity. Designers should ensure the admin interface clearly distinguishes the ELIN from regular BYOC forwarding numbers and prevents accidental assignment.
key_points: Required for emergency services during survivability mode
Configured per Site, must not be assigned to any user or entity
Separate from call forwarding BYOC numbers

concepts.title: Source-Destination Number Mapping
concepts.summary: The pre-configured forwarding logic that defines which Zoom native numbers (sources) redirect to which BYOC numbers (destinations) during an outage. Multiple source numbers can map to a single destination number. Sources can come from Users, Common Area Phones, Share Line Groups, Call Queues, and Auto Receptionists, making this a flexible but complex mapping interface.
key_points: Many-to-one mapping: multiple source numbers can forward to single destination
Sources can be from Users, Common Area Phones, Share Line Groups, Call Queues, Auto Receptionists
Default forwarding status is disabled; must be individually enabled
Main company number cannot be used as a source number

user_roles.title: Account Owner / Administrator
user_roles.summary: The only role that can configure call forwarding mappings and enable forwarding during an outage. This places a critical burden on admins during emergency situations -- they must be able to quickly access the portal and execute the enable workflow. Designers should prioritize discoverability and speed of the enable action.
key_points: Configures forwarding rules before outage (planning phase)
Manually enables forwarding during outage (emergency phase)
Requires account owner or admin privileges with edit access

task_flows.title: Configure Call Forwarding Numbers
task_flows.summary: A pre-outage planning workflow where admins define the source-to-destination number mappings. The interface uses an Add dialog with Source Number(s) and Destination Number selectors. With support for multiple source types (Users, Common Area Phones, Share Line Groups, Call Queues, Auto Receptionists), the source selector must handle grouped/filtered selection. The disabled-by-default state means admins should be prompted to review and enable individual entries after saving.
key_points: Navigate: Phone System Management > Company Info > Site > Settings > Zoom Node > Call Forwarding Local Survivability > Manage > Add
Select Source Number(s) from multiple entity types, then Destination Number from BYOC pool
Multiple source numbers can map to one destination
Default forwarding status is disabled after save; must be explicitly enabled
Main company number excluded from source selection

task_flows.title: Enable Call Forwarding During Outage
task_flows.summary: The emergency-time workflow where admins activate pre-configured forwarding rules. The interface provides a Select All option for bulk enablement, which is critical for time-sensitive outage scenarios. This flow must be extremely fast and reliable since admins may be under pressure during an actual outage.
key_points: Navigate: Phone System Management > Company Info > Site > Settings > Zoom Node > Call Forwarding Local Survivability > Manage
Click Enable Forwarding to activate
Select All option available for bulk enablement
Executed during live outage -- speed and reliability are critical

constraints.title: Site-Scoped BYOC Number Restriction
constraints.summary: Zoom native numbers can only be forwarded to BYOC numbers assigned to users within the same Site being configured. This site-scoping constraint means the destination number selector must filter available BYOC numbers by site membership. The main company number is also excluded from forwarding configuration entirely.
key_points: BYOC destination numbers must be assigned to users within the same site
Main company number cannot be configured as a forwarding source
Source numbers must be associated with the site being configured

constraints.title: Network Infrastructure Separation Requirement
constraints.summary: PSTN services and the local site internet connection must use separate network infrastructure. If both share the same local loop, an outage will affect both simultaneously, defeating the purpose of survivability. This is a critical infrastructure constraint that should be communicated during setup.
key_points: PSTN and internet must use separate physical network infrastructure
Shared local loop causes simultaneous failure during outages
Administrator must verify this separation during deployment planning

constraints.title: Account and Subscription Prerequisites
constraints.summary: Call Forwarding Local Survivability requires Business/Education/Enterprise account tier, Zoom Node and Zoom Phone subscriptions, admin privileges, and a deployed ZPLS module. These are consistent across all ZPLS features, indicating a shared eligibility gate in the admin experience.
key_points: Business, Education, or Enterprise account
Zoom Node and Zoom Phone subscriptions required
ZPLS service module must be deployed

ux_patterns.title: Two-Phase Call Forwarding Pattern (Configure then Enable)
ux_patterns.summary: Call forwarding uses a two-phase design: pre-configure mappings during planning, then manually enable during outage. This separation is necessary because forwarding rules are complex to set up and should not be rushed during an emergency. However, the enable action must be immediately accessible and support bulk operations (Select All) for rapid activation.
key_points: Planning phase: Add/Save forwarding mappings (complex, pre-outage)
Emergency phase: Enable Forwarding (simple, one-click with bulk option)
Select All for bulk enable acknowledged as design pattern for emergency speed
Clear visual distinction between configured-but-disabled and actively-forwarding states

ux_patterns.title: Manage Pattern for Site-Level Feature Configuration
ux_patterns.summary: The consistent pattern of navigating to a Site, opening Settings, and finding Zoom Node sub-features with a 'Manage' link is repeated across all call forwarding interactions. This establishes the Site Settings page as a hub for all ZPLS-related features, with Manage links acting as drill-down entry points to feature-specific configuration panels.
key_points: Site > Settings > Zoom Node serves as hub for ZPLS features
'Manage' link is the consistent entry point for detailed configuration
Consistent across configure and enable workflows
